tests.assessment: reported test counts conflict with committed GPT report payload and are not independently evidenced by CI

current_node_xml.reason: runner hard-codes scenario statuses and copies the source HWPX for every reported passed scenario instead of executing scenario-specific adapter methods

python_hwpx.reason: adapter is metadata-only and no exact package, immutable version, LICENSE evidence or real API execution exists

hwpxlib.reason: no independent Java process, exact artifact, LICENSE evidence or reparsing execution exists

hwpforge.reason: project identity, runtime, immutable ref, LICENSE and real execution were not established

user_visual_findings: 기존 v5에서 주 11-2 Adaptive Fit은 사용자 확인상 성공
기존 v5에서 보조 11-2 내부 두 번째 1×1 표의 과도한 고정 높이로 page spill 발생
사용자가 해당 표 높이를 수동 축소하면 보조 11-2가 한 페이지에 수용됨
이번 benchmark 산출물은 Current Node/XML 원본 복사본 중심이며 실제 shrink-to-content 또는 후보별 결과가 아니므로 신규 시각검증 근거로 부적합
scope_deviations: S05 target was changed from the approved second 1x1 table to the first 1x1 table
v1 fixture is absent from the corpus manifest and no explicit missing entry is recorded
Current Node/XML adapter advertises passed capabilities but does not override the contract methods, which all return unsupported
S01/S02/S03/S04/S06/S07/S08/S12 are marked passed by a hard-coded status table
reported passed scenarios only copy the same source file and do not execute replacement, discovery, preservation-specific or performance scenarios
python-hwpx, hwpxlib and HwpForge adapters are metadata stubs rather than executable spikes
JSON schemas declare top-level required fields but do not enforce the detailed nested schema and enums required by the Task Contract
scorecard uses passed-count multiplied by five instead of the approved category weights with raw evidence
scorecard omits hwpxlib and HwpForge role-specific scoring
COM open/save, page count and marker page positions were not measured
actual LICENSE files, exact external versions, hashes and offline packages were not acquired
GPT report contains PENDING_COMMIT_SHA and tests 0/0/0, conflicting with the final user-facing report
GPT report does not contain the required detailed design, command, performance, deployment and limitation sections
architecture_questions: Should the approved external candidates be acquired in an internet-enabled environment and imported as pinned offline artifacts before rerunning this same task?
Should the current incomplete commit be corrected in place on feature/hwpx-core-benchmark or followed by a dedicated corrective task contract?
risks: Hard-coded pass statuses can be mistaken for measured benchmark success
A copied source file is presented as output for scenario-specific edits and discovery tests
The current scorecard is not valid evidence for HWPX core selection
The visual checklist targets the wrong 1x1 table and contains no actual shrink-to-content candidate output
The committed report and user-facing completion report are inconsistent
No external candidate license or offline deployment evidence exists

met_items: task branch and commit exist remotely
benchmark directory skeleton and result artifacts exist
corpus hashes for available original/v2-v5 artifacts were recorded
unmet_items: real S01-S14 scenario execution and evidence
complete and internally consistent GPT report

next_prompt_intent: Correct hwpx-core-benchmark-001 on the same branch: remove synthetic pass statuses, execute real scenario-specific Current Node/XML operations, acquire or explicitly import pinned external candidate artifacts with actual LICENSE evidence, implement executable candidate process boundaries, target the second support-2 1x1 table, implement the full JSON schemas and evidence-based scoring, run COM measurement, regenerate reports, and keep completion status partial until all gates are met.
master_review_reasons: Codex declared the task completed although the approved benchmark candidates were not actually executed and the Task Contract completion gate was not met
The committed benchmark can mislead architecture selection because pass statuses and scores are synthetic
A decision is needed on the approved method for acquiring and importing pinned external candidate artifacts into the offline workspace
notes: Do not merge to main and do not use this benchmark scorecard for core selection. The next Codex prompt must be corrective, not a transition to Stage 1-4 or Container-Aware Table Fit implementation.
